name: 2026-06-05 Political Commentary Procurement Reform vs Prosecutions TH
description:
Hindi tayo dapat sanay sa mediocre.

Arrests are not reform. The pipeline that funneled nearly ₱100 billion in flood-control money to just 15 contractors is still standing.

Beneficial-ownership disclosure. Real-time procurement monitoring. Contractor concentration limits. None of that is law yet.

Jail one set of names. The system that produced them is hiring.

Arrests are not reform. The pipeline that funneled nearly ₱100 billion in flood-control money to just 15 contractors is still standing.

Beneficial-ownership disclosure. Real-time procurement monitoring. Contractor concentration limits. None of that is law yet.

Jail one set of names. The system that produced them is hiring.

Hindi tayo dapat sanay sa mediocre.

Arrests are not reform. The pipeline that funneled nearly ₱100 billion in flood-control money to just 15 contractors is still standing.

Beneficial-ownership disclosure. Real-time procurement monitoring. Contractor concentration limits. None of that is law yet.

Jail one set of names. The system that produced them is hiring.

June 3, 2026: Four governance and business groups (ICD, ISA, JRI, MAP) issued a joint statement warning that prosecutions alone will not stop the next flood-control scandal. They called for full implementation of NGPA transparency, beneficial-ownership and digital monitoring provisions; integration of SEC beneficial-ownership disclosure rules and HARBOR with procurement, tax, audit and AML systems; and public real-time monitoring of major infrastructure projects (disbursements, variation orders, cost overruns, delays, contractor concentration). Sources: Manila Bulletin (2026-06-03), Manila Times (2026-06-04), Inquirer Business. Underlying scale: about ₱100B to just 15 contractors since 2012 (~20% of total flood-control program) per Inquirer, GMA, Bangkok Post.
